name: TACTIVE-U: An Interventional Safety and Efficacy Phase 1b/2, Open-Label Umbrella Study to Investigate Tolerability, PK, and Antitumor Activity of Vepdegestrant (ARV-471/PF-07850327), an Oral Proteolysis Targeting Chimera, in Combination With Other Anticancer Treatments in Participants Aged 18 Years and Over With ER+ Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer, Sub-Study A (ARV-471 in Combination With Abemaciclib)

titles.text: TACTIVE-U: An Interventional Safety and Efficacy Phase 1b/2, Open-Label Umbrella Study to Investigate Tolerability, PK, and Antitumor Activity of Vepdegestrant (ARV-471/PF-07850327), an Oral Proteolysis Targeting Chimera, in Combination With Other Anticancer Treatments in Participants Aged 18 Years and Over With ER+ Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer, Sub-Study A (ARV-471 in Combination With Abemaciclib)

TACTIVE-U: An Umbrella Study to Investigate the Safety and Antitumor Activity of Vepdegestrant (ARV-471) in Combination With Other Medicines in Adults With ER+ Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer, Sub-Study A (ARV-471 and Abemaciclib)

The Emergency Contact Card is being replaced by a study information card and will no longer be referenced.

amendments.summary: The main purpose of the amendment is to update the end of study definition to allow treatment to continue for participants still receiving a benefit from the study drug. This amendment also introduces a reduced SoA allowing most clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the treating physician.

primaryReason.otherReason: The main purpose of the amendment is to update the end of study definition to allow treatment to continue for participants still receiving a benefit from the study drug. This amendment also introduces a reduced SoA allowing most clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the treating physician.

amendments.summary: The protocol was mainly amended to introduce in the Phase 1b portion of the study a Lead-In period with abemaciclib monotherapy and a more intensive PK sample collection to evaluate any impact of ARV-471 on the PK of abemaciclib and to allow the investigation of up to 2 dose levels for abemaciclib.

primaryReason.otherReason: The protocol was mainly amended to introduce in the Phase 1b portion of the study a Lead-In period with abemaciclib monotherapy and a more intensive PK sample collection to evaluate any impact of ARV-471 on the PK of abemaciclib and to allow the investigation of up to 2 dose levels for abemaciclib.

versions.rationale: This study aims to evaluate tolerability, pharmacokinetics (PK), and antitumor activity of vepdegestrant (ARV-471/PF-07850327) in combination with abemaciclib in adults aged 18 years and over with ER+ advanced or metastatic breast cancer.

population.name: Main Population
population.label: Participants must be patients with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with at least 1 and no more than 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease including 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen. The current A/MBC disease must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST V1.1.

notes.text: includesHealthySubjects inferred as false because this is a disease-specific (oncology) protocol.

cohorts.name: 1 (Starting dose)
cohorts.label: ARV-471 200 mg QD; Abemaciclib 150 mg BID

cohorts.name: -1A
cohorts.label: ARV-471 100 mg QD; Abemaciclib 150 mg BID

cohorts.name: -1B
cohorts.label: ARV-471 200 mg QD; Abemaciclib 100 mg BID
ARV-471 100 mg QD; Abemaciclib 100 mg BID

population.description: Participants must be patients with ER+/HER2- A/MBC, who have been previously treated with at least 1 and no more than 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease including 1 line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen. The current A/MBC disease must have at least 1 measurable lesion as per RECIST V1.1.

arms.name: ARV-471 and abemaciclib
arms.label: ARV-471 and abemaciclib
arms.description:
For the purposes of this protocol, study intervention refers to ARV-471 and abemaciclib.
The screening period will occur within 28 days prior to first dose. Study intervention will be administered in 28-day cycles.
The starting dose level of ARV-471 will be 200 mg QD and should be administered with food. The starting dose of abemaciclib will be 150 mg BID, administered with or without food.
Dosage Level(s) 200 mg QD, 100 mg QD; 150 mg BID, 100 mg BID, 50 mg BID

studyDesigns.name: Prospective, open-label, multicenter, Phase 1b/2 sub-study (C4891006 sub-study A)
studyDesigns.label: C4891006 (sub-study A) is a prospective, open-label, multicenter, Phase 1b/2 sub-study to evaluate the safety, tolerability, antitumor activity, and PK of ARV-471 with abemaciclib in the treatment of participants with A/MBC.

epochs.name: Screening
epochs.label: Screening
epochs.description: The screening period will occur within 28 days prior to first dose.

epochs.name: Combination Treatment Phase 1 Cycle = 28 days
epochs.label: Combination Treatment Phase 1 Cycle = 28 days
epochs.description: Study intervention will be administered in 28-day cycles. The starting dose level of ARV-471 will be 200 mg QD and should be administered with food. The starting dose of abemaciclib will be 150 mg BID, administered with or without food. Dose modifications may occur in 1 of 3 ways: Within a cycle: dosing interruption until adequate recovery and dose reduction, if required, during a given treatment cycle; Between cycles: dosing interruptions may continue due to persisting toxicity when a new cycle is due to start; In the next cycle: dose reduction may be required based on toxicity experienced in the previous cycle.

epochs.name: Post-Treatment Follow-Up
epochs.label: Post-Treatment Follow-Up
epochs.description: Follow-up visit will be performed at least 28 days (+7 days) from last dose of study intervention OR prior to the initiation of any new anticancer therapy, whichever comes first. The SAE reporting period is 28 days from last dose regardless of the initiation of a new anticancer therapy (see Section 8.4.1).

epochs.name: Survival Follow-up
epochs.label: Survival follow-up (Phase 2 only)
epochs.description: For Phase 2, survival status will be collected in the CRF every 3 months (±7 days) from the last dose of study treatment, including subsequent anticancer therapies (eg, start and stop dates, BOR and date of PD of the subsequent therapy), until death, withdrawal of consent, lost to follow-up, or end of study, whichever occurs first. Telephone contact is acceptable.

elements.name: EL2
elements.label: Allocation to Study Intervention
elements.description: Allocation of participants to treatment groups will proceed using an IRT system. The site personnel (study coordinator or specified designee) will be required to enter select information including, but not limited to, the user's ID and password, the protocol number, and the participant number. The site personnel will then be provided with a treatment assignment, and DU or container number when study intervention is being supplied via the IRT system. The IRT system will provide a confirmation report containing the participant number, and DU or container number assigned. The confirmation report must be stored in the site's files. Allocation to study intervention must be completed after eligibility has been confirmed. Study intervention administration should start ≤3 days after assignment.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule_Allocation
transitionEndRule.text: Study intervention administration should start ≤3 days after assignment.

elements.name: EL1
elements.label: Informed Consent Process
elements.description: The investigator or the investigator's representative will explain the nature of the study, including the risks and benefits, to the participant or their legally authorized representative and answer all questions regarding the study. The investigator may not delegate obtaining consent to a local healthcare professional. The participant or their legally authorized representative should be given sufficient time and opportunity to ask questions and to decide whether or not to participate in the trial. Participants must be informed that their participation is voluntary. Participants or their legally authorized representative (if allowed by local regulations) will be required to sign a statement of informed consent that meets the requirements of 21 CFR 50, local regulations, ICH GCP guidelines, privacy and data protection requirements, where applicable, and the IRB/EC or study center. The medical record must include a statement that written informed consent was obtained before the participant was enrolled in the study and the date on which the written consent was obtained. The authorized person obtaining the informed consent must also sign the ICD. Participants or their legally authorized representative must be reconsented to the most current IRB/EC version of the IRB/EC-approved ICD(s) during their participation in the study as required per local regulations. A copy of the ICD(s) must be provided to the participant or their legally authorized representative (if allowed by local regulations). Participants who are rescreened are required to sign a new ICD.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule_InformedConsent
transitionEndRule.text: Informed consent must be obtained within 28 days of enrollment and prior to any protocol-required assessments being performed (Section 10.1.3).

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule_InformedConsent
transitionStartRule.text: the time the participant provides informed consent, which is obtained before undergoing any study-related procedure and/or receiving study intervention

elements.name: EL3
elements.label: Study intervention administration
elements.description: Study intervention will be administered in 28-day cycles. The starting dose level of ARV-471 will be 200 mg QD and should be administered with food. The starting dose of abemaciclib will be 150 mg BID, administered with or without food. Dose modifications may occur in 1 of 3 ways: Within a cycle: dosing interruption until adequate recovery and dose reduction, if required, during a given treatment cycle; Between cycles: dosing interruptions may continue due to persisting toxicity when a new cycle is due to start; In the next cycle: dose reduction may be required based on toxicity experienced in the previous cycle.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule_Treatment
transitionEndRule.text: It may be necessary for a participant to permanently discontinue study intervention.

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule_Treatment
transitionStartRule.text: Study intervention administration should start ≤3 days after assignment.

elements.name: EL4
elements.label: Post-Treatment Follow-up
elements.description: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first), participants will return to site to undergo the safety follow-up assessments as reported in the SoA. Participants continuing to experience AEs at this point following discontinuation of treatment will continue to be followed at least every 4 weeks until resolution or determination, in the clinical judgment of the investigator, that no further improvement is expected.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule_PostTreatmentFollowUp
transitionEndRule.text: Participants continuing to experience AEs at this point following discontinuation of treatment will continue to be followed at least every 4 weeks until resolution or determination, in the clinical judgment of the investigator, that no further improvement is expected.

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule_PostTreatmentFollowUp
transitionStartRule.text: At least 28 calendar days (+7 days) after discontinuation of study intervention or prior if a new anticancer therapy is initiated (whichever comes first), participants will return to site to undergo the safety follow-up assessments as reported in the SoA.

elements.name: EL5
elements.label: Survival Follow-up (Phase 2 only)
elements.description: For Phase 2, survival status will be collected in the CRF every 3 months (±7 days) from the last dose of study treatment, including subsequent anticancer therapies (eg, start and stop dates, BOR and date of PD of the subsequent therapy), until death, withdrawal of consent, lost to follow-up, or end of study, whichever occurs first. Telephone contact is acceptable.

transitionEndRule.name: EndRule_SurvivalFollowUp
transitionEndRule.text: until death, withdrawal of consent, lost to follow-up, or end of study, whichever occurs first.

transitionStartRule.name: StartRule_SurvivalFollowUp
transitionStartRule.text: every 3 months (±7 days) from the last dose of study treatment

estimands.name: Primary Estimand - Phase 1b DLT rate
estimands.description: DLT rate estimated based on data from DLT-evaluable participants during the DLT observation period (Cycle 1).
estimands.populationSummary: DLT-evaluable participants in Phase 1b who received ≥1 dose and either experienced DLT(s) during Cycle 1 or completed the DLT observation period without DLT; participants who received <75% of planned dose for non–treatment-related reasons are not evaluable.

estimands.name: Primary Estimand - Phase 2 ORR
estimands.description: The treatment effect of ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib on ORR per RECIST v1.1 response criteria as determined by investigator assessment.
estimands.populationSummary: Participants with advanced/metastatic ER+/HER2- breast cancer who received ≥1 dose of study intervention (Phase 2 analysis population: SAS).

intercurrentEvents.name: Subsequent anticancer therapy
intercurrentEvents.text: All data collected after an intercurrent event of subsequent anticancer therapy will be excluded.

intercurrentEvents.name: Missing postbaseline assessment / early PD or death
intercurrentEvents.text: Participants who do not have a postbaseline disease assessment due to early PD, who receive anticancer therapies other than the study intervention prior to achieving an objective response, or who die, experience documented PD, or stop disease assessments for any reason prior to achieving an objective response will be counted as nonresponders.

studyDesigns.rationale: TACTIVE-U is an umbrella study comprising multiple sub-studies that independently evaluate ARV-471 in participants with ER+ A/MBC. ARV-471 will act as the backbone therapy given in combination with other anticancer agents thought to have clinical relevance in ER+ breast cancer (see Section 4.2.3).

activities.name: GnRH or LHRH Agonist (for Men and Pre/Perimenopausal Participants)
activities.label: GnRH or LHRH Agonist (for Men and Pre/Perimenopausal Participants)

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_01
objectives.text: Assess safety and tolerability of ARV-471 combined with abemaciclib in participants with ER+/HER2- advanced/metastatic breast cancer to select a recommended Phase 2 dose (RP2D).

endpoints.name: EP_PR_01_DLT
endpoints.text: Occurrence of dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) during the Cycle 1 DLT observation period.
endpoints.description: DLTs during DLT observation period (Cycle 1).

objectives.description: To assess safety and tolerability of ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib in participants with ER+/HER2- A/MBC to select a RP2D.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_01
objectives.text: Evaluate the overall safety profile of ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_01_AEs
endpoints.text: Adverse events characterized by type, frequency, intensity, timing, seriousness, and relationship to study treatment as graded by NCI CTCAE v5.0.
endpoints.description: AEs as characterized by type, frequency, intensity as graded by NCI CTCAE version 5.0, timing, seriousness, and relationship to ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_02_Labs
endpoints.text: Laboratory test abnormalities characterized by type, frequency, severity/intensity and timing, graded by NCI CTCAE v5.0.
endpoints.description: Laboratory test abnormalities as characterized by type, frequency, intensity (as graded by NCI CTCAE version 5.0), and timing

objectives.description: To evaluate the overall safety profile.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_02
objectives.text: Evaluate antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_03_ORR_Ph1b
endpoints.text: Confirmed objective response (CR or PR) by investigator assessment.
endpoints.description: Confirmed OR (CR or PR) by investigator assessment

endpoints.name: EP_SC_04_DoR_Ph1b
endpoints.text: Duration of response (DoR) by investigator assessment for participants with confirmed OR.
endpoints.description: DoR by investigator assessment

endpoints.name: EP_SC_05_CBR_Ph1b
endpoints.text: Clinical benefit rate (CBR): confirmed CR or PR at any time, or stable disease ≥24 weeks, by investigator assessment.
endpoints.description: CBR (confirmed CR or PR at any time, or SD ≥24 weeks) by investigator assessment

endpoints.name: EP_SC_06_PFS_Ph1b
endpoints.text: Progression-free survival (PFS) by investigator assessment.
endpoints.description: PFS by investigator assessment

objectives.description: To evaluate antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_03_PK_Effect
objectives.text: Evaluate the effect of ARV-471 on the pharmacokinetics of abemaciclib and its active metabolites (M2, M18, M20).

endpoints.name: EP_SC_07_Abema_PK
endpoints.text: AUCtau and Cmax of abemaciclib and metabolites M2, M18, M20 with and without co-administration of ARV-471.
endpoints.description: AUC tau and C max of abemaciclib, M2, M18 and M20, with and without co-administration of ARV-471

objectives.description: To evaluate the effect of ARV-471 on PK of abemaciclib and its active metabolites M2, M18, M20

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_04_Plasma_Exposure
objectives.text: Evaluate plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV-473, abemaciclib and abemaciclib active metabolites (M2, M18, M20) when given in combination.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_08_Plasma_Concentrations
endpoints.text: Plasma concentrations of ARV-471, ARV-473, abemaciclib, and abemaciclib active metabolites M2, M18, and M20.
endpoints.description: Plasma concentrations of ARV-471, ARV-473, abemaciclib, and abemaciclib active metabolites M2, M18, and M20

objectives.description: To evaluate the plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV-473, abemaciclib, and abemaciclib active metabolites M2, M18, and M20, when ARV-471 and abemaciclib are given in combination

objectives.name: OBJ_EX_01_Tumor_Biology
objectives.text: Explore tumor biology and exploratory biomarkers to understand treatment effects and mechanisms of sensitivity or resistance to ARV-471.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_01_Biomarkers
endpoints.text: Measurements of biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein, defined cell types) from peripheral blood and/or tumor tissue biospecimens.
endpoints.description: Measurements of biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein, or defined cell types) resulting from analyses of peripheral blood and/or tumor tissue biospecimen.

objectives.description: To evaluate tumor biology and explore the treatment effect of ARV-471.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_02_Phase2
objectives.text: Assess the clinical antitumor activity of ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib in participants with advanced/metastatic ER+/HER2- breast cancer.

endpoints.text: Objective response rate (ORR): confirmed CR or PR per RECIST v1.1 by investigator assessment.
endpoints.description: Confirmed OR (CR or PR) determined by investigator assessment.

objectives.description: To assess the clinical antitumor activity of ARV- 471 in combination with abemaciclib.

endpoints.description: OS.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_06_Phase2_Safety
objectives.text: Further characterize the overall safety and tolerability of ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib in Phase 2.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_13_AEs_Phase2
endpoints.text: Adverse events characterized by type, frequency, intensity (NCI CTCAE v5.0), timing, seriousness, and relationship to study treatment.
endpoints.description: AEs as characterized by type, frequency, intensity as graded by NCI CTCAE, version 5.0, timing, seriousness, and relationship to ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib.  Laboratory test abnormalities as characterized by type, frequency, severity (as graded by NCI CTCAE version 5.0), and timing.

objectives.description: To further characterize the overall safety profile and tolerability of ARV-471 in combination with abemaciclib.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_07_Phase2_Plasma_PK
objectives.text: Evaluate plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV-473, abemaciclib, and abemaciclib metabolites when administered in combination in Phase 2.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_14_Plasma_Concentrations_Phase2
endpoints.text: Plasma concentrations of ARV-471, ARV-473, abemaciclib, and abemaciclib metabolites M2, M18, and M20 in Phase 2 participants.
endpoints.description: Plasma concentrations of ARV- 471, ARV-473, abemaciclib, and abemaciclib active metabolites M2, M18 and M20.

objectives.description: To evaluate the plasma exposure of ARV-471, ARV- 473, abemaciclib, and abemaciclib active metabolites M2, M18 and M20, when ARV-471 and abemaciclib are given in combination.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_08_ctDNA
objectives.text: Assess changes from baseline in plasma circulating tumor DNA (ctDNA) with treatment and potential association with clinical outcomes.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_15_ctDNA
endpoints.text: Quantitative changes in plasma ctDNA from pretreatment baseline to evaluate associations with clinical outcomes.
endpoints.description: ctDNA plasma quantitative changes from pre treatment to evaluate potential predictability of their associations with clinical outcomes.

objectives.description: To assess changes from baseline levels in plasma ctDNA with treatment.

objectives.name: OBJ_EX_02_PROs
objectives.text: Assess patient-reported symptoms and health-related quality of life when treated with abemaciclib plus ARV-471.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_02_EORTC_QLQ_C30
endpoints.text: General cancer-related symptoms measured by the EORTC-QLQ-C30.
endpoints.description: General cancer-related symptoms as measured by the EORTC-QLQ-C30.

endpoints.name: EP_EX_03_EQ5D5L
endpoints.text: General health status measured by the EQ-5D-5L instrument.
endpoints.description: General health status as measured by the EQ-5D-5L.

objectives.description: To assess the impact of abemaciclib in combination with ARV-471 on patient-reported symptoms and health-related quality of life.

objectives.name: OBJ_EX_03_TumorBiology_Phase2
objectives.text: Understand tumor biology and identify potential biomarkers indicating treatment sensitivity or resistance (eg, ESR1).

endpoints.name: EP_EX_04_Biomarker_Identification
endpoints.text: Identification of potential biomarkers that may indicate treatment sensitivity or resistance (for example, ESR1).
endpoints.description: Identification of potential biomarkers that may be indicative of treatment sensitivity and/or resistance (eg, ESR1 ).

objectives.description: To understand tumor biology of breast cancer and to identify potential sensitive and/or resistance mechanisms.

indications.name: IND1
indications.label: ER+ Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer
indications.description: Estrogen receptor positive (ER+) advanced or metastatic breast cancer

scheduleTimelines.name: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.label: Main Timeline
scheduleTimelines.description: Main timeline for the study design.
scheduleTimelines.entryCondition: Informed consent obtained (Informed consent must be obtained within 28 days of enrollment and prior to any protocol-required assessments being performed.)

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in accor
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in accordance with local regulations) at screening.

Refer to Section 10.4 for reproductive criteria for male (Section 10.4.1) and female (Section 10.4.2) participants.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Histological or cytological diagnosis of A/MBC that is not amendable to s
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Histological or cytological diagnosis of A/MBC that is not amendable to surgical resection with curative intent (≥1% ER+ stained cells as per local practice on the most recent tumor biopsy [Allison et al, 2020], HER2- tumor by IHC or in-situ hybridization per ASCO/CAP) (Wolff et al, 2018):

 Participants who have bilateral breast cancers that are both ER+/HER2- are eligible.

 Tumor block collected at the time of diagnosis with local recurrent or metastatic disease, or archival tumor tissue, is required for inclusion. Refer to Section 8.7.1.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Prior anticancer therapies:  At least 1 and no more than 2 lines of prior thera
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Prior anticancer therapies:

 At least 1 and no more than 2 lines of prior therapies for advanced/metastatic disease:

 ≤1 prior chemotherapy (no antibody-drug conjugates permitted)

 ≤2 prior endocrine-based therapies

 No prior elacestrant or experimental endocrine therapy (eg, SERD, CERAN, PROTAC, SERCA) permitted.

 1, and only 1, line of any prior CDK4/6 inhibitor-based regimen is required (independent of the setting eg, adjuvant or advanced/metastatic). If prior CDK4/6 inhibitor was permanently discontinued due to an adverse event, the participant will not be eligible. If prior CDK4/6 inhibitor required dose reductions due to adverse events, refer to Section 6.9.1 for the eligibility evaluation.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as defined by RECIST v1
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants must have at least 1 measurable lesion as defined by RECIST v1.1. Participants with bone lesions only could be included if at least 1 bone lesion has a measurable component as for RECIST v1.1 (see Section 10.12).

eligibilityCriteria.name: 7. Prior procedures and treatment:  Major surgery, radiotherapy, prior endocri
eligibilityCriteria.text:
7. Prior procedures and treatment:

 Major surgery, radiotherapy, prior endocrine therapy, CDK4/6 inhibitors, or other anticancer treatments within 14 days prior enrollment in the study (for anticancer therapy containing an antibody-based agents, approved or investigational, 28 days or 5 half-lives, whichever is shorter,).

 Participation in other clinical studies involving investigational drug(s) or vaccine(s) within 28 days or 5 half-lives prior enrollment in the study (whichever is shorter). Participants may be eligible if they are in the follow-up phase of an investigational study provided that at least 5 half-lives have elapsed from the last dose.

 Participants who received: prior radiotherapy to ≥25% of bone marrow, or prior hematopoietic stem cell, or bone marrow transplantation are not eligible independent of when it was received.

Any unresolved toxicities from prior surgeries or therapies Grade >1 at the time of study enrollment except for Grade 2 alopecia, peripheral neuropathy, arthralgia, or other toxicities not considered a safety risk for the participant per the investigator's judgment.

Any medical or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study, or any social situations that would limit study compliance.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular d
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseases, defined as:

 Any of the following in the previous 6 months: myocardial infarction, severe/unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, symptomatic congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association class III or IV), cerebrovascular accident, transient ischemic attack, or symptomatic pulmonary embolism or other clinically significant episode of thromboembolic disease.

 Symptomatic cardiac valve disease. Participants with mitral valve prolapse that is asymptomatic or not associated with clinically significant sequalae (eg, mitral regurgitation) are eligible.

 QTcF >470 msec at screening or any of the following in the previous 6 months: congenital long QT syndrome, Torsade de Pointes, clinically important arrhythmias, left anterior hemiblock (bifascicular block), ongoing cardiac dysrhythmias of Grade ≥2, atrial fibrillation of any grade. Participants with cardiac rhythm device/pacemaker must be discussed in detail with sponsor medical monitor to determine eligibility.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants in visceral crisis at risk of life-threatening complications in th
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants in visceral crisis at risk of life-threatening complications in the short term, including participants with massive uncontrolled effusions (pleural, pericardial, and/or peritoneal), pulmonary lymphangitis, and liver involvement >50%.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with a known history of drug-induced pneumonitis or other sig
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants with a known history of drug-induced pneumonitis or other significant symptomatic deterioration of lung functions.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastases, or symptomatic CNS meta
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastases, or symptomatic CNS metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease as indicated by clinical symptoms, cerebral edema, and/or progressive growth. Participants with a history of CNS metastases or cord compression are eligible if they have been definitively treated (eg, radiotherapy, stereotactic surgery) and clinically stable (including participants with residual CNS symptoms/deficits) and discontinued anti-seizure medications and corticosteroids for at least 14 days prior to enrollment.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic GI disease, GI ulcer, GI bleeding,
eligibilityCriteria.text: Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic GI disease, GI ulcer, GI bleeding, inability to swallow the formulated product, or previous significant, gastric bowel resection that would preclude adequate absorption of study interventions.

eligibilityCriteria.name: 12. Hepatic dysfunction defined as:  Total bilirubin >1.5 × ULN unless the p
13. Hematologic abnormalities defined as:  ANC <1,500/mm 3 or 1.5 × 10 9 /L;
Known active infection including HBV, HCV, and HIV or AIDS-related illness (s
eligibilityCriteria.text: Known active infection including HBV, HCV, and HIV or AIDS-related illness (screening for chronic conditions is not required). A participant who has a positive test result for SARS-CoV-2 infection, is known to have asymptomatic infection, or is suspected of having SARS-CoV-2, is not eligible for the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: History of any other malignancies within the past 3 years, except for the fo
eligibilityCriteria.text: History of any other malignancies within the past 3 years, except for the following: (1) adequately treated basal or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin; (2) curatively treated in situ carcinoma of the cervix. All other malignancies must have been curatively treated and with no evidence of disease for >3 years. Participants with inflammatory breast cancer are excluded.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Concurrent administration of medications, food, or herb supplements that a
eligibilityCriteria.text: Concurrent administration of medications, food, or herb supplements that are strong/moderate inhibitors and inducers of CYP3A, and drugs known to predispose to Torsade de Pointes or QT interval prolongation. Prior use of strong/moderate CYP3A inhibitors must be stopped 7 days and strong/moderate inducers of CYP3A must be stopped 14 days before enrollment in the study. Refer to Section 6.9 and Section 10.9 for the list of prohibited drugs.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Renal impairment defined as an eGFR <45 mL/min*. In equivocal cases, a 24-
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Renal impairment defined as an eGFR <45 mL/min*. In equivocal cases, a 24-hour urine collection test can be used to estimate the creatinine clearance more accurately.

Use the 2021 CKD-EPI eGFR (serum creatinine only) equation [see Section 10.7] to calculate eGFR (in units of mL/min/1.73m²). By multiplication of {[eGFR] X [nomogram-based participant body surface area (m 2 )} = estimated creatinine clearance (eCrCl) in mL/min. The eGFR cutoff is set to 45 mL/min based on the preliminary results of ARV-471-CPhm-103 study in healthy postmenopausal female participants indicating that the renal clearance of ARV-471 is a minor pathway representing less than 0.1% of total clearance. Moreover, as per abemaciclib prescribing information no dosage adjustment of abemaciclib is required for participants with mild or moderate renal impairment.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and the
eligibilityCriteria.text: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and sponsor and sponsor delegate employees directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members.

studyInterventions.name: ARV-471
studyInterventions.description: Investigational PROTAC ER degrader evaluated as the protocol agent and backbone therapy; administered orally in 28-day cycles.

administrations.name: ARV-471 200 mg QD — 28-day cycle
administrations.description: Must be administered with food. When given in combination with abemaciclib, take ARV-471 first in the morning followed by abemaciclib.

administrations.name: ARV-471 100 mg QD — 28-day cycle
administrations.description: Dose reduction level; must be administered with food.

administrableProducts.name: ARV-471 tablets
administrableProducts.description: 100 mg tablets provided in a high-density polyethylene bottle with child-resistant cap.

substance.name: ARV-471
substance.description: PROTAC small molecule that induces degradation of the estrogen receptor (ER).

studyInterventions.name: abemaciclib
studyInterventions.description: CDK4/6 inhibitor administered orally as part of the combination; continuous dosing on 28-day cycles with a possible 7-day PK lead-in.

administrations.name: abemaciclib 150 mg BID — 28-day cycle

duration.text: Continuous on 28-day cycles; 7-day PK Lead-In when applicable

administrations.description: Administered orally twice daily (every 12 hours) continuously on 28-day cycles; first dose on Day -1 must be taken with food. When given in combination, abemaciclib may be taken with or without food except as noted; second daily dose should be taken in the evening.

administrations.name: abemaciclib 100 mg BID — 28-day cycle

duration.text: Continuous on 28-day cycles; 7-day PK Lead-In when applicable

administrations.description: Administered orally twice daily (every 12 hours) continuously on 28-day cycles; first dose on Day -1 must be taken with food when applicable.

administrations.name: abemaciclib 50 mg BID — 28-day cycle

duration.text: Continuous on 28-day cycles; 7-day PK Lead-In when applicable

administrations.description: Administered orally twice daily (every 12 hours) continuously on 28-day cycles; first dose on Day -1 must be taken with food when applicable.

administrableProducts.name: abemaciclib tablets (Verzenio)
administrableProducts.description: Tablets provided in blister pack; available as 50 mg, 100 mg, and 150 mg strengths.

substance.name: abemaciclib
substance.description: Selective oral inhibitor of cyclin-dependent kinases 4 and 6 (CDK4/6 inhibitor).
